Mechanical Engineer focused on CAD design, manufacturing, and hands-on problem solving. Experienced in SolidWorks, flow simulation, aluminum sand casting, and mechanical system troubleshooting. Interested in manufacturing, design, or testing engineering roles where engineering decisions directly impact performance and reliability.

FEATURED PROJECTS

01
AcroBot — Aerial Robotics Senior Capstone
Aug 2024 – May 2025 · Cal State LA · Team Captain

Autonomous acrobatic robot designed to perform repeatable free-fall stunts — inspired by Walt Disney Imagineering's Spider-Man attraction. Led a cross-disciplinary ME + EE team as captain, responsible for all mechanical design and CAD, with fabrication/assembly handled by team members.

AcroBot suspended mid-flight during testing
Pneumatic backpack unit
Cylinder & CO₂ assembly

Chest enclosure
Electronics housing

Tucked position

$297.97
Total Cost
  • Team Captain — led design direction, coordinated cross-disciplinary team; handled all mechanical CAD and occasional 3D printing; fabrication and assembly executed by teammates
  • Designed all structural components and enclosures in SolidWorks/Onshape: chest housing, bar grip mechanism, pneumatic backpack, limb geometry, and rigging mounts
  • Bar grip uses an electromagnet to lock the trapeze bar — cutting power releases springs that pull the slider back instantly for clean autonomous release
  • Pneumatic servo drives a cylinder + U-joint system for tuck motion; CO₂ backpack tank pressure monitored via gauge on cover
  • Hollow PLA thighs (6 lb) and feet (~1.2 lb each) fillable with sand or ball bearings to fine-tune balance and flip performance
  • Ran FEA on high-stress regions; reduced total system mass by ~12% through geometry optimization and material selection
  • All requirements met: bar grip release, leash release, autonomous release, and IMU-based orientation sensing (BNO055)

02
PicassoBot — 3-DOF Drawing Robot
Jan 2025 – May 2025 · Intro to Robotics

A 3-DOF robotic arm programmed to autonomously draw continuous paths. Focused on kinematics, trajectory planning, and physical prototype redesign.

  • Programmed full kinematics in MATLAB using Denavit–Hartenberg parameters and inverse kinematics
  • Developed a path-smoothing algorithm for continuous drawing; reduced trajectory error to <5%
  • Rebuilt prototype structure to improve modularity, accessibility, and mechanical reliability

03
Machined Aluminum Flashlight
Jan 2024 – May 2024 · Manufacturing Processes

Designed and fully machined a functional 4-inch aluminum flashlight housing from raw stock, applying precision manufacturing techniques from drawing to assembled product.

  • Created detailed manufacturing drawings with GD&T to define tolerances and fits
  • Machined housing via lathe and mill: internal boring, threading, and countersinking operations
  • Held key fits within ±0.005 in. tolerance to ensure proper assembly of LED, lens, and switch
  • Delivered a fully functional flashlight integrating LED, switch, and batteries in a sealed housing
CNC Lathe & Mill GD&T SolidWorks Precision Tolerancing Manufacturing

04
Aluminum Sand Casting Foundry
Jan 2026 – Present · Personal / Ongoing

Designed and operated a small-scale aluminum casting foundry to manufacture custom metal parts using sand casting. Built a propane-fired furnace capable of reaching 700–900 °C, melting aluminum alloys, and producing functional cast components from open sand molds. Engineering emphasis on thermal system design, material behavior, and iterative process improvement.

Aluminum pellets — charge material for melting and casting
Furnace interior at temperature — aluminum melting
Solidified aluminum ingot — graphite mold
First open sand cast — "Casa de Rosas" aluminum plaque
660 °C
Melt Point
2.7 g/cm³
Density
~6–7%
Shrinkage
700–900 °C
Furnace Temp
  • Furnace system: Propane burner + steel shell + ceramic fiber insulation + refractory crucible — engineered for heat retention, crucible stability, and safe burner angle/airflow
  • Melting process: Charge aluminum scrap → heat to ~660 °C → skim oxide slag (dross) → pour into prepared mold; managed heat loss during transfer and oxide formation throughout
  • Sand casting: Pattern pressed into Petrobond/green sand to form drag cavity; mold compacted with vent paths and flow channels; open pour directly into mold
  • First cast part: Decorative aluminum plaque with raised lettering — defects identified included incomplete fill in thin sections, surface roughness from sand grain size, and oxide inclusions; improved through better sand packing and gating
  • Iteration & next steps: Currently progressing to cope-and-drag closed molds for improved geometry control; future plans include CNC/3D printed patterns and transition to bronze casting
Sand Casting Foundry Ops Thermal Systems Materials Science DFM Process Iteration In Progress

05
Impact Biomechanics — Injury Criteria Analysis
Apr 2025 · Cal State LA

Simulated vehicle collision scenarios to assess occupant injury risk under varying safety conditions — no seatbelt, standard restraints, and delayed airbag deployment.

  • Modeled crash scenarios in MADYMO and extracted HIC, BRIC, and Nij injury metrics
  • Mapped injury values to AIS (Abbreviated Injury Scale) severity levels for clinical context
  • Analyzed how crash dynamics and restraint setups affected neck loads, head accelerations, and rotational kinematics
MADYMO Impact Kinematics Crash Analysis AIS / HIC / Nij Simulation

06
Heat Transfer: Sauna Thermal Analysis
Dec 2024 · Cal State LA

Applied transient and steady-state thermal modeling to design and analyze a functional sauna system, optimizing for heating performance, comfort, and energy efficiency.

  • Built a detailed 3D SolidWorks model of the sauna to support thermal simulation
  • Simulated conduction, convection, and radiation using MATLAB (ODE45) differential equations
  • Evaluated material properties, insulation effects, and boundary conditions to optimize the design

07
Team Project Calendar — Production Web App
Mar 2026 – Present · Personal / Deployed

A fully custom, production-deployed scheduling and job management system built for a real painting contracting company. Currently in active use by admin and field workers daily. ~4,170 lines of code shipped as a single HTML file.

  • Built drag-and-drop team calendar with month/week views, multi-day job blocks, and per-worker color coding
  • Implemented full role-based auth (Admin/Employee), password reset flows, and worker notification feeds
  • Cloud sync via Railway backend; offline fallback via base64-compressed JSON share codes
  • Fully bilingual UI (English/Spanish) designed for a real-world bilingual workforce
  • Built with React (CDN, no build step), CSS variables, useReducer state management — zero dependencies
